title: AI-901 Mock Exam 1: Concepts, Models, and Foundry
description: A 50-question unofficial mock paper aligned to the AI-901 skills measured from April 15, 2026. Uses realistic dropdown, statement, single-choice, and multiple-answer formats with original questions and official Microsoft Learn references.

domains.title: Implement AI solutions by using Microsoft Foundry

items.prompt: A loan-ranking model approves equally qualified applicants at different rates across demographic groups. What should the team investigate first?
items.answer: Whether training data and evaluation results reveal unfair performance differences
options: Whether training data and evaluation results reveal unfair performance differences
Whether response latency is identical in every region
Whether the model uses the maximum token limit
items.explanation: Fairness requires measuring whether comparable people receive comparable quality and outcomes. Regional latency and formatting do not diagnose unequal model performance.

items.prompt: A hospital uses an AI recommendation only after a clinician reviews the evidence and can reject it. Which principle does this control support most directly?
items.explanation: Accountability keeps people and organizations responsible for consequential decisions. A reviewer with authority provides oversight; the other choices describe accessibility or technical workloads.

items.prompt: {{blank}} is supported by giving users clear notice that an answer was generated by AI.
items.explanation: Transparency helps people understand when AI is involved and what its limitations are. Elasticity is an infrastructure property, while clustering and speech recognition are AI techniques.

items.prompt: For each proposed control, decide whether it directly supports responsible deployment.

statements.text: Test safety behavior with representative high-risk prompts before release
Assume a high benchmark score removes the need for production monitoring
Provide no route for users to challenge consequential outcomes

items.explanation: Pre-release evaluation, ongoing monitoring, and meaningful recourse are lifecycle controls. A benchmark is evidence, not a guarantee, and removing recourse weakens responsible deployment.

items.prompt: Which TWO actions most directly improve privacy and security for an app that sends customer records to a model?
answers: Remove unnecessary personal fields before requests are sent
Restrict access to prompts, outputs, and logs
options: Remove unnecessary personal fields before requests are sent
Restrict access to prompts, outputs, and logs
Publish raw prompts for unrestricted troubleshooting
items.explanation: Data minimization reduces exposed information, while access controls protect retained data. Sampling temperature does not secure data, and publishing raw prompts expands exposure.

items.prompt: Which TWO design choices make an AI service more inclusive?
answers: Test the experience with people who have varied abilities
Provide interaction alternatives such as text and speech
options: Test the experience with people who have varied abilities
Provide interaction alternatives such as text and speech
Use one language and input method for consistency
Measure only the average response time
items.explanation: Inclusive design considers a range of abilities and contexts and offers usable alternatives. A single interaction path and an average latency metric do not establish accessibility.

items.prompt: A support assistant must answer from frequently updated internal manuals and cite the relevant passages. Which approach should be evaluated first?
items.answer: Ground the model with retrieval from the approved manuals
options: Ground the model with retrieval from the approved manuals
Fine-tune the model every time a manual changes
Raise temperature so the model recalls more facts
Use image generation to create citations
items.explanation: Retrieval supplies current approved material at request time and can preserve source links. Fine-tuning is not an efficient document-update mechanism, and temperature or image generation cannot add verified knowledge.

items.prompt: What does a token represent when a generative model processes a prompt?
items.answer: A unit of text processed by the model
options: A unit of text processed by the model
A user identity stored in Microsoft Entra ID
A confidence score for an extracted field
items.explanation: Tokens are the text units a model consumes and produces when calculating context and output limits. They are unrelated to identity tokens, extraction confidence, or compute instances.

items.prompt: Two models meet an accuracy target, but one costs less and responds faster under the expected load. What is the best deployment choice?
items.answer: Use the lower-cost, lower-latency model after representative evaluation
options: Use the lower-cost, lower-latency model after representative evaluation
Always deploy the model with the largest parameter count
Select whichever model appears first in the catalog
Increase the slower model’s maximum output tokens
items.explanation: Model choice should balance measured quality, latency, throughput, and cost for the workload. Size, catalog order, and larger outputs do not by themselves improve suitability.

items.prompt: To limit how long a generated response can become, configure the {{blank}} parameter.
items.explanation: Maximum output tokens sets an upper bound on generated length. Temperature affects sampling variation, while embedding dimensions and content-filter categories serve different purposes.

items.prompt: Evaluate each statement about generative-model configuration.

statements.text: Lower temperature generally makes sampling more consistent
Grounding permanently changes the model weights
A model should be evaluated on representative inputs before deployment

items.explanation: Temperature influences output variation, and evaluation measures suitability on the intended workload. Grounding adds context at request time rather than modifying model weights.

items.prompt: Which TWO requirements most strongly indicate that a multimodal model is needed?
answers: The request includes a photograph that must be interpreted
The response must relate visual evidence to written instructions
options: The request includes a photograph that must be interpreted
The response must relate visual evidence to written instructions
The response must contain fewer than 100 tokens
The client uses environment variables for configuration
items.explanation: Multimodal capability is required when a model must reason over visual and textual inputs together. Output length and application configuration do not determine input modality.

items.prompt: A retailer predicts the numeric value of next month’s sales from historical data. Which machine-learning task is this?
items.explanation: Regression predicts a continuous numeric value such as sales. Classification predicts categories, clustering discovers groups, and object detection locates items in images.

items.prompt: A system assigns incoming messages to billing, technical support, or sales. Which task is being performed?
items.explanation: Multiclass classification selects one category from several possible labels. The required output is neither numeric, pixel-level, nor synthesized audio.

items.prompt: A manufacturer has no failure labels and wants to discover unusual sensor readings. Which technique fits the requirement?
items.explanation: Anomaly detection finds observations that depart from learned normal patterns and does not require labeled failure classes. The other techniques solve different output problems.

items.prompt: A photo-processing system must return the location of every vehicle as rectangular coordinates. Which vision capability is required?
items.explanation: Object detection identifies instances and returns their locations, commonly as bounding boxes. Classification labels a whole image, OCR reads text, and sentiment analyzes language.

items.prompt: A call center needs written transcripts of customer conversations. Which AI capability supplies the required first output?
items.explanation: Speech recognition converts spoken audio into text that downstream analytics can process. Synthesis performs the reverse, while key phrases and captions operate on other inputs.

items.prompt: An app must extract merchant, tax, and total fields from varied receipt images. Which workload best describes the requirement?
items.explanation: The desired output is a defined set of fields grounded in source images, which is structured extraction. The task does not create an image, infer opinion, or discover unlabeled groups.

items.prompt: {{blank}} finds organization names and locations in a news article.
items.explanation: Named entity recognition finds and categorizes mentions such as organizations and places in text. The alternatives operate on audio, images, or numeric prediction.

items.prompt: For each scenario, decide whether the named workload is appropriate.

statements.text: Use summarization to produce a short account of a long report
Use sentiment analysis to find every person named in an email
Use supervised classification to discover groups when no labels exist

items.explanation: Summarization condenses content. Named entity recognition—not sentiment—finds people in text, and clustering—not supervised classification—discovers groups when labels are absent.

items.prompt: Which TWO outputs are characteristic of an object-detection solution?
answers: A class label for each detected object
options: A class label for each detected object
A sentiment score for the whole image
A spoken version of the image file name
items.explanation: Object detection identifies what each object is and where it appears. Sentiment and speech output are unrelated to locating visual objects.

items.prompt: Which TWO capabilities are required for a kiosk that accepts spoken questions and reads answers aloud?
items.explanation: A voice interaction needs speech-to-text processing for spoken input and text-to-speech processing for audible output. OCR and segmentation process visual data.

items.prompt: A team wants to configure instructions, a model, and file search in the Foundry portal without maintaining custom orchestration code. What should it create?
options: A hosted agent with custom packages
items.explanation: A prompt agent is configured declaratively with instructions, a model, and tools. Hosted agents are for custom orchestration, while analyzers and gateways solve different problems.

items.prompt: A Python agent uses a custom orchestration framework and extra packages. The team wants Foundry-managed hosting and observability. Which agent type fits?
options: A prompt agent with no custom runtime
items.explanation: Hosted agents run custom code and dependencies in a managed Agent Service environment. Prompt agents do not provide an arbitrary custom runtime, and the other services are not agent hosts.

items.prompt: Which prompt role should contain enduring instructions about an assistant’s behavior and boundaries?
items.explanation: System instructions establish persistent behavior and constraints for the conversation. User content and retrieved data should not replace higher-priority instructions, and deployment names are configuration.

items.prompt: An agent needs to answer from uploaded product manuals. Which built-in tool is the most direct fit?
items.explanation: File search retrieves relevant passages from uploaded knowledge sources for grounded answers. The other tools execute code or create audio or images rather than search documents.

items.prompt: An agent must calculate totals and produce a chart from an uploaded CSV file. Which tool should be added?
items.explanation: Code interpreter can execute code over uploaded data for calculations and charts. Retrieval and language or speech analysis alone do not perform the requested computation.

items.prompt: Before an agent executes a money-transfer function, what is the most important application control?
items.answer: Validate the arguments and require authorization or confirmation
options: Validate the arguments and require authorization or confirmation
Raise temperature to explore alternative transfers
Hide the tool call from audit logs
Grant the model unrestricted administrator access
items.explanation: High-impact tools need validated inputs, scoped authorization, and often explicit confirmation. Greater randomness, reduced auditability, or excessive privilege increases rather than controls risk.

items.prompt: Use {{blank}} to authenticate a local Foundry SDK client without embedding a key in source code.
items.explanation: DefaultAzureCredential can obtain a Microsoft Entra token from supported development or managed environments. Public, anonymous, or prompt-embedded secrets are not safe authentication patterns.

items.prompt: To compare model responses interactively before writing client code, begin in the Foundry {{blank}}.
items.explanation: A Foundry playground supports interactive prompt and model testing before application integration. Billing, DNS, and resource locks do not provide model inference experiments.

items.prompt: Evaluate each statement about building a Foundry application.

statements.text: Representative evaluations should be run before replacing a production model
Retrieved text should be allowed to override system instructions
Tracing can help diagnose model and tool steps

items.explanation: Evaluation and tracing support controlled releases and diagnosis. Retrieved content is untrusted data and must not be permitted to replace higher-priority application instructions.

items.prompt: Which TWO components are needed by a lightweight client that calls a deployed model securely?
answers: The correct project or model endpoint
A supported credential with appropriate access
options: The correct project or model endpoint
A supported credential with appropriate access
A password embedded in browser JavaScript
An image analyzer for every text request
items.explanation: A client needs the correct endpoint and an authorized credential. Embedding secrets in a browser is unsafe, and a visual analyzer is unnecessary for text-only requests.

items.prompt: Which text-analysis capability returns whether customer feedback is positive, neutral, or negative?
items.explanation: Sentiment analysis estimates expressed opinion or polarity in text. Entity recognition finds named things, translation works with languages, and detection locates visual objects.

items.prompt: A company wants the most important nouns and phrases from thousands of support messages. Which capability should it use?
items.explanation: Key phrase extraction identifies salient words and phrases in text. The alternatives create audio, label images, or predict numeric values.

items.prompt: A meeting recording must be transcribed asynchronously after it ends. Which Speech capability is most appropriate?
items.explanation: Batch transcription is designed for processing stored audio asynchronously. Synthesis creates audio, while image and sentiment workloads do not transcribe speech.

items.prompt: Converting generated text into audible narration uses speech {{blank}}.
items.explanation: Speech synthesis converts text into spoken audio. Recognition converts audio to text, while clustering and segmentation solve unrelated analytical tasks.

items.prompt: Evaluate each statement about language and speech capabilities.

statements.text: Entity recognition can identify people and organizations in text
Speech synthesis converts an audio recording into a transcript
Language detection translates text into every supported language

items.explanation: Entity recognition finds named things. Speech recognition—not synthesis—produces a transcript, and language detection identifies a language without performing translation.

items.prompt: Which TWO features can improve a speech-recognition solution for specialist vocabulary and multi-speaker meetings?
answers: A phrase list or custom speech model for domain terms
options: A phrase list or custom speech model for domain terms
An Azure Policy initiative for punctuation
items.explanation: Vocabulary adaptation can improve recognition of domain terms, and diarization separates speakers. Image resolution and governance initiatives do not change transcription behavior.

items.prompt: A quality-control app must decide whether an entire image shows a cracked component. Which capability is sufficient?
items.explanation: Image classification assigns a label to the whole image, which fits a single cracked-or-not result. Bounding boxes are needed only when locations or multiple instances matter.

references.title: Image inputs with Azure OpenAI models

items.prompt: A user submits a photograph and asks a model to explain the visible safety hazard. What model capability is required?
items.answer: Visual input understanding in a multimodal model
options: Visual input understanding in a multimodal model
items.explanation: The model must interpret visual evidence together with a text instruction, requiring multimodal visual understanding. Text embeddings, audio generation, and forecasting do not inspect images.

references.title: Image inputs with Azure OpenAI models

items.prompt: A design tool must create a new product illustration from a written description. Which workload should it use?
items.explanation: Image generation creates new visual content from a prompt. OCR reads existing text, detection locates existing objects, and sentiment evaluates language.

references.title: Image inputs with Azure OpenAI models

items.prompt: {{blank}} returns the text printed on a photographed shipping label.
items.explanation: Optical character recognition extracts visible text from an image. Translation, generation, and regression do not perform visual text recognition.

references.title: Image inputs with Azure OpenAI models

items.prompt: Evaluate each statement about computer-vision outputs.

statements.text: Object detection can return a bounding box for each detected item
Image classification must return the coordinates of every object
A multimodal model can combine an image with a written prompt

items.explanation: Detection includes object locations and multimodal models can use visual and textual inputs. Classification normally labels an image and does not require per-object coordinates.

references.title: Image inputs with Azure OpenAI models

items.prompt: Which prebuilt Content Understanding analyzer is the most direct starting point for common invoice fields?
options: A prompt agent with web search
items.explanation: A prebuilt invoice analyzer is designed to extract established invoice structure. Speech, web search, and image generation do not provide grounded invoice field extraction.

items.prompt: A custom extraction solution must return policy number, claimant, and loss date. What should the analyzer define?
items.answer: A schema containing those fields and clear descriptions
options: A schema containing those fields and clear descriptions
items.explanation: A field schema tells Content Understanding which structured values to extract, and descriptive definitions improve accuracy. Sampling, voices, and tool permissions do not define output structure.

items.prompt: Why should an app retain source locations returned with extracted fields?
items.answer: They let reviewers trace values back to supporting content
options: They let reviewers trace values back to supporting content
They increase the model context window
items.explanation: Source locations provide grounding and review evidence for extracted values. They do not change context limits, security responsibilities, or language automatically.

items.prompt: A company needs searchable descriptions for a large image archive before indexing it. Which Content Understanding capability is relevant?
items.answer: An image analyzer that produces descriptive content
options: An image analyzer that produces descriptive content
An audio analyzer configured for diarization
A classification model with no image input
items.explanation: An image analyzer can interpret visual content and return descriptions suitable for downstream indexing. Audio, speech output, or a model without visual input cannot describe the images.

items.prompt: An analyzer returns a low-confidence total from a damaged receipt. What should the workflow do?
items.answer: Route the field for validation according to the business threshold
options: Route the field for validation according to the business threshold
Treat every returned value as certain
Raise temperature until the value changes
items.explanation: Confidence should inform validation and escalation rules, especially for consequential or poor-quality inputs. It is not a guarantee, and repeatedly changing sampling does not verify the source.

items.prompt: Content Understanding uses an analyzer {{blank}} to define the structured fields an application needs.
items.explanation: An analyzer schema defines field names, types, and descriptions for structured extraction. Voices, subnets, and temperature are unrelated to the requested output contract.

items.prompt: Evaluate each statement about multimodal extraction.

statements.text: Content Understanding can process document, image, audio, and video inputs
A required schema guarantees that the source contains every field
Grounding information makes review of extracted values unnecessary

items.explanation: Content Understanding supports several content modalities and can return grounding evidence for review. Neither a schema nor grounding guarantees that every value is present or correct.
